outcomes.title: APP - Trig EQ
outcomes.description: Apply trigonometric equations to solve problems.

data.desc: The ocean tide at a specific harbor is modeled by the equation below, where <m>d(t)</m> represents the water's height in feet above mean sea level after <m>t</m> hours.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the tide is <m>10\sqrt{3}</m> feet above mean sea level.
data.outtro:
10\sqrt{3} = 20 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6} t\right)

\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6} t\right)

\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{6} t

\frac{\pi}{6} t = \frac{1}{3} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{6} t = \frac{2}{3} \, \pi

t = 2 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 4

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{6}{\pi} = 12

\boxed{t = 2, 4, 14, 16, \dots}

data.desc: A weight attached to a spring is pulled and then released. After <m>t</m> seconds, its vertical displacement <m>d(t)</m> in inches is given by the equation below, where a positive value indicates the weight is above its resting equilibrium.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the weight is <m>2</m> inches above its resting equilibrium.
data.outtro:
2 = 4 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{18} t\right)

\frac{1}{2} = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{18} t\right)

\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{18} t

\frac{\pi}{18} t = \frac{1}{6} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{18} t = \frac{5}{6} \, \pi

t = 3 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 15

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{18}{\pi} = 36

\boxed{t = 3, 15, 39, 51, \dots}

data.desc: A weight attached to a spring is pulled and then released. After <m>t</m> seconds, its vertical displacement <m>d(t)</m> in inches is given by the equation below, where a positive value indicates the weight is above its resting equilibrium.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the weight is <m>9\sqrt{3}</m> inches above its resting equilibrium.
data.outtro:
9\sqrt{3} = 18 \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} = \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

\cos^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{12} t

\frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{1}{6} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{11}{6} \, \pi

t = 2 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 22

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{12}{\pi} = 24

\boxed{t = 2, 22, 26, 46, \dots}

data.desc: The ocean tide at a specific harbor is modeled by the equation below, where <m>d(t)</m> represents the water's height in feet above mean sea level after <m>t</m> hours.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the tide is <m>3\sqrt{2}</m> feet above mean sea level.
data.outtro:

t = 2 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 6

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{8}{\pi} = 16

\boxed{t = 2, 6, 18, 22, \dots}

data.desc: A buoy bobs up and down on the ocean waves. Its vertical displacement is modeled by the equation below, where <m>d(t)</m> represents the buoy's height in feet above the calm water level after <m>t</m> seconds.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the buoy is <m>3</m> feet above the calm water level.
data.outtro:
3 = 6 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6} t\right)

\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{6} t

t = 1 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 5

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{6}{\pi} = 12

\boxed{t = 1, 5, 13, 17, \dots}

\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2} = \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{8} t\right)

\cos^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{8} t

\frac{\pi}{8} t = \frac{1}{4} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{8} t = \frac{7}{4} \, \pi

t = 2 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 14

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{8}{\pi} = 16

\boxed{t = 2, 14, 18, 30, \dots}

data.desc: A weight attached to a spring is pulled and then released. After <m>t</m> seconds, its vertical displacement <m>d(t)</m> in inches is given by the equation below, where a positive value indicates the weight is above its resting equilibrium.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the weight is <m>4\sqrt{2}</m> inches above its resting equilibrium.
data.outtro:
4\sqrt{2} = 8 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2} = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{12} t

\frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{1}{4} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{3}{4} \, \pi

t = 3 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 9

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{12}{\pi} = 24

\boxed{t = 3, 9, 27, 33, \dots}

\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2} = \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{8} t\right)

t = 2 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 14

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{8}{\pi} = 16

\boxed{t = 2, 14, 18, 30, \dots}

data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the buoy is <m>7\sqrt{3}</m> feet above the calm water level.
data.outtro:
7\sqrt{3} = 14 \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{18} t\right)

\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} = \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{18} t\right)

\cos^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{18} t

\frac{\pi}{18} t = \frac{1}{6} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{18} t = \frac{11}{6} \, \pi

t = 3 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 33

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{18}{\pi} = 36

\boxed{t = 3, 33, 39, 69, \dots}

t = 1 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 5

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{3}{\pi} = 6

\boxed{t = 1, 5, 7, 11, \dots}

data.desc: A weight attached to a spring is pulled and then released. After <m>t</m> seconds, its vertical displacement <m>d(t)</m> in inches is given by the equation below, where a positive value indicates the weight is above its resting equilibrium.

\boxed{t = 2, 22, 26, 46, \dots}

6\sqrt{3} = 12 \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{6} t\right)

\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} = \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{6} t\right)

\cos^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{6} t

\frac{\pi}{6} t = \frac{1}{6} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{6} t = \frac{11}{6} \, \pi

t = 1 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 11

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{6}{\pi} = 12

\boxed{t = 1, 11, 13, 23, \dots}

data.desc: A weight attached to a spring is pulled and then released. After <m>t</m> seconds, its vertical displacement <m>d(t)</m> in inches is given by the equation below, where a positive value indicates the weight is above its resting equilibrium.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the weight is <m>7\sqrt{2}</m> inches above its resting equilibrium.
data.outtro:
7\sqrt{2} = 14 \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{4} t\right)

\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2} = \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{4} t\right)

\cos^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{4} t

\frac{\pi}{4} t = \frac{1}{4} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{4} t = \frac{7}{4} \, \pi

t = 1 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 7

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{4}{\pi} = 8

\boxed{t = 1, 7, 9, 15, \dots}

data.desc: A buoy bobs up and down on the ocean waves. Its vertical displacement is modeled by the equation below, where <m>d(t)</m> represents the buoy's height in feet above the calm water level after <m>t</m> seconds.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the buoy is <m>7\sqrt{3}</m> feet above the calm water level.
data.outtro:
7\sqrt{3} = 14 \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{18} t\right)

\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} = \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{18} t\right)

\cos^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{18} t

\frac{\pi}{18} t = \frac{1}{6} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{18} t = \frac{11}{6} \, \pi

t = 3 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 33

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{18}{\pi} = 36

\boxed{t = 3, 33, 39, 69, \dots}

data.desc: A weight attached to a spring is pulled and then released. After <m>t</m> seconds, its vertical displacement <m>d(t)</m> in inches is given by the equation below, where a positive value indicates the weight is above its resting equilibrium.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the weight is <m>3</m> inches above its resting equilibrium.
data.outtro:
3 = 6 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

\frac{1}{2} = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{12} t

\frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{1}{6} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{5}{6} \, \pi

t = 2 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 10

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{12}{\pi} = 24

\boxed{t = 2, 10, 26, 34, \dots}

data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the buoy is <m>10\sqrt{2}</m> feet above the calm water level.

t = 2 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 14

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{8}{\pi} = 16

\boxed{t = 2, 14, 18, 30, \dots}

5 = 10 \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{6} t\right)

\frac{1}{2} = \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{6} t\right)

\cos^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{6} t

\frac{\pi}{6} t = \frac{1}{3} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{6} t = \frac{5}{3} \, \pi

t = 2 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 10

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{6}{\pi} = 12

\boxed{t = 2, 10, 14, 22, \dots}

data.desc: A buoy bobs up and down on the ocean waves. Its vertical displacement is modeled by the equation below, where <m>d(t)</m> represents the buoy's height in feet above the calm water level after <m>t</m> seconds.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the buoy is <m>2\sqrt{2}</m> feet above the calm water level.
data.outtro:
2\sqrt{2} = 4 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2} = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{12} t

\frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{1}{4} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{3}{4} \, \pi

t = 3 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 9

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{12}{\pi} = 24

\boxed{t = 3, 9, 27, 33, \dots}

data.desc: A weight attached to a spring is pulled and then released. After <m>t</m> seconds, its vertical displacement <m>d(t)</m> in inches is given by the equation below, where a positive value indicates the weight is above its resting equilibrium.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the weight is <m>7\sqrt{3}</m> inches above its resting equilibrium.
data.outtro:
7\sqrt{3} = 14 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{12} t

\frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{1}{3} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{2}{3} \, \pi

t = 4 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 8

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{12}{\pi} = 24

\boxed{t = 4, 8, 28, 32, \dots}

\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

t = 4 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 8

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{12}{\pi} = 24

\boxed{t = 4, 8, 28, 32, \dots}

data.desc: A buoy bobs up and down on the ocean waves. Its vertical displacement is modeled by the equation below, where <m>d(t)</m> represents the buoy's height in feet above the calm water level after <m>t</m> seconds.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the buoy is <m>4\sqrt{3}</m> feet above the calm water level.
data.outtro:
4\sqrt{3} = 8 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{3} t\right)

\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{3} t\right)

\frac{\pi}{3} t = \frac{1}{3} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{3} t = \frac{2}{3} \, \pi

t = 1 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 2

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{3}{\pi} = 6

\boxed{t = 1, 2, 7, 8, \dots}

\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{9} t\right)

\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{9} t

\frac{\pi}{9} t = \frac{1}{3} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{9} t = \frac{2}{3} \, \pi

t = 3 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 6

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{9}{\pi} = 18

\boxed{t = 3, 6, 21, 24, \dots}

data.desc: The ocean tide at a specific harbor is modeled by the equation below, where <m>d(t)</m> represents the water's height in feet above mean sea level after <m>t</m> hours.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the tide is <m>5</m> feet above mean sea level.
data.outtro:
5 = 10 \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{6} t\right)

\frac{1}{2} = \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{6} t\right)

\cos^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{6} t

\frac{\pi}{6} t = \frac{1}{3} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{6} t = \frac{5}{3} \, \pi

t = 2 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 10

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{6}{\pi} = 12

\boxed{t = 2, 10, 14, 22, \dots}

data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the weight is <m>9</m> inches above its resting equilibrium.
data.outtro:
9 = 18 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

\frac{1}{2} = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{12} t

\frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{1}{6} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{5}{6} \, \pi

t = 2 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 10

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{12}{\pi} = 24

\boxed{t = 2, 10, 26, 34, \dots}

\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2} = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{8} t\right)

\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{8} t

\frac{\pi}{8} t = \frac{1}{4} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{8} t = \frac{3}{4} \, \pi

t = 2 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 6

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{8}{\pi} = 16

\boxed{t = 2, 6, 18, 22, \dots}

data.desc: A weight attached to a spring is pulled and then released. After <m>t</m> seconds, its vertical displacement <m>d(t)</m> in inches is given by the equation below, where a positive value indicates the weight is above its resting equilibrium.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the weight is <m>5</m> inches above its resting equilibrium.
data.outtro:
5 = 10 \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

\frac{1}{2} = \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

\cos^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{12} t

\frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{1}{3} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{5}{3} \, \pi

t = 4 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 20

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{12}{\pi} = 24

\boxed{t = 4, 20, 28, 44, \dots}

data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the weight is <m>9\sqrt{2}</m> inches above its resting equilibrium.
data.outtro:
9\sqrt{2} = 18 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{8} t\right)

\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2} = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{8} t\right)

\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{8} t

\frac{\pi}{8} t = \frac{1}{4} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{8} t = \frac{3}{4} \, \pi

t = 2 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 6

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{8}{\pi} = 16

\boxed{t = 2, 6, 18, 22, \dots}

5 = 10 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

\frac{1}{2} = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{12} t

\frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{1}{6} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{5}{6} \, \pi

t = 2 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 10

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{12}{\pi} = 24

\boxed{t = 2, 10, 26, 34, \dots}

data.desc: The ocean tide at a specific harbor is modeled by the equation below, where <m>d(t)</m> represents the water's height in feet above mean sea level after <m>t</m> hours.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the tide is <m>9\sqrt{2}</m> feet above mean sea level.
data.outtro:
9\sqrt{2} = 18 \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{4} t\right)

\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2} = \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{4} t\right)

\cos^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{4} t

\frac{\pi}{4} t = \frac{1}{4} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{4} t = \frac{7}{4} \, \pi

t = 1 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 7

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{4}{\pi} = 8

\boxed{t = 1, 7, 9, 15, \dots}

data.desc: A buoy bobs up and down on the ocean waves. Its vertical displacement is modeled by the equation below, where <m>d(t)</m> represents the buoy's height in feet above the calm water level after <m>t</m> seconds.

3\sqrt{3} = 6 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

\frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{1}{3} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{2}{3} \, \pi

t = 4 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 8

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{12}{\pi} = 24

\boxed{t = 4, 8, 28, 32, \dots}

2\sqrt{2} = 4 \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{8} t\right)

\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2} = \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{8} t\right)

\cos^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{8} t

\frac{\pi}{8} t = \frac{1}{4} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{8} t = \frac{7}{4} \, \pi

t = 2 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 14

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{8}{\pi} = 16

\boxed{t = 2, 14, 18, 30, \dots}

data.desc: The ocean tide at a specific harbor is modeled by the equation below, where <m>d(t)</m> represents the water's height in feet above mean sea level after <m>t</m> hours.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the tide is <m>5</m> feet above mean sea level.
data.outtro:
5 = 10 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6} t\right)

\frac{1}{2} = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6} t\right)

\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{6} t

\frac{\pi}{6} t = \frac{1}{6} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{6} t = \frac{5}{6} \, \pi

t = 1 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 5

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{6}{\pi} = 12

\boxed{t = 1, 5, 13, 17, \dots}

data.desc: A weight attached to a spring is pulled and then released. After <m>t</m> seconds, its vertical displacement <m>d(t)</m> in inches is given by the equation below, where a positive value indicates the weight is above its resting equilibrium.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the weight is <m>10</m> inches above its resting equilibrium.
data.outtro:
10 = 20 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{18} t\right)

\frac{1}{2} = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{18} t\right)

\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{18} t

\frac{\pi}{18} t = \frac{1}{6} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{18} t = \frac{5}{6} \, \pi

t = 3 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 15

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{18}{\pi} = 36

\boxed{t = 3, 15, 39, 51, \dots}

data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the weight is <m>2\sqrt{3}</m> inches above its resting equilibrium.
data.outtro:
2\sqrt{3} = 4 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{3} t\right)

\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{3} t\right)

\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{3} t

\frac{\pi}{3} t = \frac{1}{3} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{3} t = \frac{2}{3} \, \pi

t = 1 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 2

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{3}{\pi} = 6

\boxed{t = 1, 2, 7, 8, \dots}

data.desc: A weight attached to a spring is pulled and then released. After <m>t</m> seconds, its vertical displacement <m>d(t)</m> in inches is given by the equation below, where a positive value indicates the weight is above its resting equilibrium.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the weight is <m>9</m> inches above its resting equilibrium.
data.outtro:
9 = 18 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{18} t\right)

\frac{1}{2} = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{18} t\right)

\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{18} t

\frac{\pi}{18} t = \frac{1}{6} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{18} t = \frac{5}{6} \, \pi

t = 3 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 15

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{18}{\pi} = 36

\boxed{t = 3, 15, 39, 51, \dots}

data.desc: A buoy bobs up and down on the ocean waves. Its vertical displacement is modeled by the equation below, where <m>d(t)</m> represents the buoy's height in feet above the calm water level after <m>t</m> seconds.
data.task: Find all values of <m>t</m> for which the buoy is <m>3\sqrt{2}</m> feet above the calm water level.
data.outtro:
3\sqrt{2} = 6 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12} t\right)

\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{12} t

\frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{1}{4} \, \pi \quad \text{OR} \quad \frac{\pi}{12} t = \frac{3}{4} \, \pi

t = 3 \quad \text{OR} \quad t = 9

\text{Period} = 2\pi \cdot \frac{12}{\pi} = 24

\boxed{t = 3, 9, 27, 33, \dots}
